message:
Implement speculative cancel-correct streaming pipeline with evidence ledger and file processing

Major expansion of the speculation engine, streaming pipeline, and
orchestrator to deliver the full speculative cancel-correct workflow:

speculation.rs:
- Switch CalibrationTracker from Vec to VecDeque for O(1) eviction
  instead of O(n) Vec::remove(0) on every rolling-window update
- Add confirmations_emitted field to SpeculationStats for parity
  with the robot event contract
- Refactor WindowManager::next_window into create_window helper to
  share window construction logic between bounded and unbounded APIs
- Add WindowManager::next_window_bounded() for final-window truncation
  that returns None when audio_position >= max_end, preventing
  zero-length window materialization at source
- Add WindowManager::set_window_size() with min/max clamping
- Add CorrectionEvidenceLedger with bounded VecDeque storage for
  per-window evidence trails including fast/quality model latencies,
  drift metrics, controller confidence, and fallback state
- Add CorrectionEvidenceEntry struct with full decision context
- Add ledger diagnostics: correction_rate, mean latencies, mean WER,
  latency savings percentage, window size trend
- Add capacity-zero retention mode (count-only, no storage)
- Add is_correction_decision() with normalized string matching for
  "correct", "corrected", "correction" variants (trim + lowercase)
- Fix minor formatting in fallback_active branch
- Add comprehensive unit tests: window boundary/duration, correction
  drift computation, beta posterior updates, calibration tracker
  Brier score and eviction, evidence ledger capacity-zero and
  eviction behavior, bounded window edge cases, correction tracker
  unregistered window errors, all_resolved transitions

streaming.rs:
- Remove to_model_segment() bridge function that caused precision-lossy
  round-trips (model -> backend -> model) turning confidence:None into
  Some(0.0) and truncating sub-ms timestamps
- Capture original model segments directly via Arc<Mutex> holders in
  bridge closures, using backend segments only for executor transport
- Extract process_window_by_id() helper to share window processing
  logic between single-window and duration-loop APIs
- Add push_event() helper for consistent RunEvent construction
- Add full event emission: transcript.partial (per-segment from fast
  model), transcript.confirm, transcript.retract, transcript.correct
  using canonical robot payload builders
- Add process_duration_loop() internal method implementing
  deterministic window advancement with bounded final windows,
  cancellation checkpoint hooks, and speculation_stats summary event
- Add process_duration_with_models() public API with checkpoint +
  model runner callbacks
- Add process_duration_with_models_no_checkpoint() convenience wrapper
- Add process_file_with_models() that probes audio duration via
  ffprobe and drives the duration loop
- Add build_result() and stats() with confirmations_emitted field
- Add comprehensive unit tests: backend segment conversion, config
  defaults, fresh pipeline empty result, confirm/correct paths,
  zero-duration immediate return, event sequence contiguity,
  emit_events=false suppression, retract+correct event emission

orchestrator.rs:
- Fix comment: "warned but still run" replaces "logged and skipped"
  for finalizer budget documentation accuracy
- Add NOTE comment directing speculative mode callers to
  SpeculativeStreamingPipeline instead of single-backend path
- Add recommended_budget zero service_ms floor clamping test
- Add punctuate_segments already-punctuated no-changes note test
- Add PipelineCx cancel/uncancel round-trip test
- Add VAD energy detect below min_voice_ratio silence_only test
- Add stage_latency_profile terminal-only event skip test

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
